evidence: PR #14603 Fix ElidePermutations pass in the presence of PermutationGates
reproduction_command: build baseline+candidate via build_qiskit_event then python -m cart.cli historical-run --event hist-elide-permutations-mapping
expected_observable_signal: Candidate transpile of a PermutationGate circuit is NOT semantically equivalent to source (and/or wrong TranspileLayout); baseline equivalent
notes: REJECTED (production-unobservable): targeted trigger gave pass on buggy candidate via full preset PM + semantic oracle (backend none & line, opt3). Real per PR #14603 but corrupts virtual_permutation_layout property, not the layout-applied unitary. Needs property-level/isolated-pass oracle in a retrospective track (deviation).

reproduction_command: build baseline+candidate via build_qiskit_event then python -m cart.cli historical-run --event hist-final-layout-composition
expected_observable_signal: Wrong composed final_layout/routing_permutation after >1 final_layout-setting pass -> semantic mismatch; baseline correct

evidence: PR #14730 Fix non-determinism VF2Layout with only 1q gates
reproduction_command: build baseline+candidate via build_qiskit_event then python -m cart.cli historical-run --event hist-vf2layout-determinism-1q
expected_observable_signal: Repeated fixed-seed candidate runs give varying layout -> unstable 2Q/depth; baseline stable
notes: BLOCKED (not reproduced): determinism oracle (op-counts+depth equal across 2 runs) returned pass on buggy build; op-counts/depth are invariant under layout permutation. A stronger fingerprint oracle (gate order + qubit indices + final_layout) was then implemented and the buggy build STILL passed: PR #14730's non-determinism is tied to a noisy Target (GenericBackendV2 noise scoring), which our coupling-map-only backend does not exercise. Remains blocked; retrospective trigger needs a noisy Target.

evidence: PR #14120 added VF2PostLayout at L3 (no-op; reverted in 2.1.2; issue #14867)
reproduction_command: build baseline+candidate via build_qiskit_event then python -m cart.cli historical-run --event hist-vf2postlayout-noop
notes: higher median transpile time at L3 vs baseline (Stage-1 perf screening); semantics+quality unchanged
column19: BLOCKED (perf below threshold): all pass on 4 small GHZ units (unit-limit 4); no-op overhead < 20% screen and single-sample timing is under-powered vs the 3-run median in S2.5. Exploratory; deferred to Phase 6 per S2.5. A 16-unit retry (incl. large opt3 circuits) also returned all pass -> overhead stays sub-threshold with single-sample timing; needs 3-run median + controlled hardware (Phase 6).
